original_hypothesis.title: GearBoxV3._getPositionTVL Uses Magic address(840) for USD Pricing, Likely Causing TVL Revert and Vault DoS
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: GearBoxV3._getPositionTVL calls _getValue(address(840), base, amount) using address(840) (0x348) as a pseudo-token address for USD denomination. If the Noya Value Oracle has no price feed mapping for address(840) -> baseToken, the call reverts, causing TVLHelper.getTVL to revert and DoS-ing the entire vault.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: address(840) is a non-standard magic number for USD. No deployment script configures the value oracle with a price feed for this address. The Noya Value Oracle would need an explicit mapping from address(0x348) to the vault's baseToken, which is a fragile and undocumented requirement.
deployment_assumptions: Value oracle must have a configured price feed for address(840) to baseToken
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment script in the provided set configures a price feed for address(840). deploySharedContracts.ts creates NoyaValueOracle but doesn't show oracle feed configuration.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _getValue(address(840), base, ...) uses hardcoded magic address for USD denomination

preconditions: A Gearbox V3 credit account holding position exists in the vault
Value oracle does not have a price feed for address(840)

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Oracle revert propagates to TVLHelper.getTVL, blocking all vault operations
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: DoS prevents withdrawals; depositors' funds locked until Gearbox position is removed

economic_questions: Does the Noya Value Oracle support address(840) as a valid token?
What happens to vault operations when TVL calculation reverts?
Can the Gearbox position be removed to unblock the vault?
validation_requirements: Test TVL calculation with an active Gearbox position

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: 0 (exploit is triggered by normal vault operations)
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Indirect: vault frozen, time-value loss for depositors
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Persistent until oracle configured or position removed

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: All deposits and withdrawals blocked for the vault

validation_result.mechanism: Magic address oracle dependency causing TVL revert DoS
validation_result.impact_summary: GearBoxV3._getPositionTVL uses address(840) as a pseudo-USD token when calling the value oracle. If the oracle has no price feed for this magic address, the call reverts, propagating through TVLHelper.getTVL and blocking all vault operations (deposits, withdrawals, fee calculations).
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The GearBoxV3 connector's _getPositionTVL function calls _getValue(address(840), base, amount) where address(840) = 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000348 is a Gearbox V3 protocol convention for USD denomination. The _getValue function in BaseConnector checks if token == baseToken (which is unlikely since baseToken would be USDC, WETH, etc., not address(840)), and if the amount is non-zero, it calls valueOracle.getValue(address(840), baseToken, amount). The NoyaValueOracle must have an explicit price feed configured for address(840) to the vault's baseToken. If this feed is absent, the oracle call reverts. Since TVLHelper.getTVL iterates ALL holding positions in a single call without try/catch, a single reverting position DoS-es the entire vault's TVL calculation. No deployment script in the provided set configures the oracle with a price feed for address(840).
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Direct evidence found in GearBoxV3.sol showing the hardcoded address(840) usage in _getPositionTVL. BaseConnector._getValue shows no fallback for unknown tokens - it directly calls the oracle. TVLHelper.getTVL has no try/catch around individual position TVL calculations, confirming revert propagation.

vulnerable_code_snippets.line_numbers: Last function in contract, approximately lines 73-84
vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: address(840) is a magic number representing USD in Gearbox V3 protocol. This non-standard address is passed to the Noya value oracle which requires an explicit price feed mapping. Without configuration, the oracle call reverts.

No try/catch or fallback when oracle doesn't support the token address. When token is address(840) and baseToken is e.g., USDC, this calls valueOracle.getValue(address(840), USDC, amount) which will revert if no price feed exists.
No try/catch around getPositionTVL calls. A single reverting position (GearBoxV3 with unconfigured oracle) causes the entire getTVL to revert, blocking all vault operations.

calling_sequence: TVLHelper.getTVL loops through all holding positions
_getPositionTVL computes (d.totalValueUSD - d.totalDebtUSD) and calls _getValue(address(840), base, amount)
BaseConnector._getValue checks token != baseToken and amount != 0, then calls valueOracle.getValue(address(840), baseToken, amount)
Oracle reverts due to missing price feed for address(840)
Revert propagates up through TVLHelper.getTVL, blocking all vault operations
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: TVLHelper.getTVL -> GearBoxV3._getPositionTVL (line: return _getValue(address(840), base, ...)) -> BaseConnector._getValue -> valueOracle.getValue(address(840), baseToken, amount) -> REVERT. The revert propagates because TVLHelper.getTVL has no try/catch around individual connector TVL calls. Any AccountingManager function that calls TVL() will also revert, including deposit processing, withdrawal processing, and fee calculations.

deployment_assumptions_checked: Value oracle must have a configured price feed for address(840) to baseToken
config_validation.deployment_evidence: deploySharedContracts.ts creates NoyaValueOracle with only the registry address as constructor parameter. No deployment script in the provided set configures any price feeds for the oracle, let alone one for address(840). The deployValueOracle function simply calls: NoyaValueOracle.deploy(registryAddress) with no subsequent feed configuration.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an active attack but a configuration/code defect. The scenario unfolds as follows: (1) A Gearbox V3 credit account is opened via openAccount(), creating a holding position in the registry. (2) Any subsequent call to TVLHelper.getTVL (triggered by deposits, withdrawals, TVL queries, fee calculations) iterates all positions including the Gearbox one. (3) If the value oracle lacks a price feed for address(840)->baseToken, the _getPositionTVL call reverts. (4) The revert propagates through the entire TVL calculation. (5) All vault operations depending on TVL are blocked. No capital or special timing is needed - the DoS is triggered by normal vault operations as soon as a Gearbox position exists and has non-zero net value.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity DoS. When triggered: (1) All vault deposits cannot be processed (TVL needed for share price). (2) All vault withdrawals are blocked (TVL needed for share calculation). (3) Management and performance fees cannot be calculated. (4) Cross-chain TVL updates fail. The entire vault is effectively frozen. Recovery requires either: (a) configuring the oracle with an address(840) price feed, (b) closing the Gearbox position via closeAccount (which doesn't require TVL calculation), or (c) updating the value oracle contract. During the DoS period, all depositor funds are locked.
recommended_mitigations: Replace address(840) with a proper USD-denominated stablecoin address or use the Gearbox credit manager's underlying token for price conversion
Add try/catch in TVLHelper.getTVL around individual getPositionTVL calls to prevent a single failing position from blocking the entire vault
Document the oracle configuration requirement for address(840) and add deployment-time validation
Add a check in GearBoxV3._getPositionTVL: if valueOracle doesn't support address(840), fall back to returning 0 with an event
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy PositionRegistry, NoyaValueOracle (without configuring address(840) feed), and GearBoxV3 connector. 2. Mock or use a real Gearbox V3 facade/credit manager. 3. Open a Gearbox credit account via openAccount(). 4. Deposit some tokens into the credit account so totalValueUSD > totalDebtUSD > 0. 5. Call TVLHelper.getTVL() and observe the revert. 6. Verify that AccountingManager operations (deposit, withdraw) also revert.
uncertainties: The NoyaValueOracle implementation is not in scope - its exact behavior for unknown tokens is inferred (most oracle implementations revert for unsupported pairs)
It's possible that in production deployment, the oracle IS configured for address(840) via a separate non-provided script or manual transaction
The Gearbox credit manager's calcDebtAndCollateral behavior and return values depend on external protocol state
additional_testing_needed: Test NoyaValueOracle.getValue behavior when called with address(840) as the token parameter
Integration test: deploy full stack including Gearbox connector and verify TVL calculation with and without oracle configuration for address(840)
Verify that closeAccount can be called even when TVL calculation is broken (it doesn't depend on TVL)
